channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South Africa’s New Labour Reforms Could Improve Rights for Gig Economy Workers
item.description: South Africa’s Minister of Employment and Labour is working on major changes to key labour laws, including the Basic Conditions of Employment Act and the National Minimum Wage Act, to extend better pr...

item.title: Investigation into Seatbelt Use After Fatal Sydney Crash
item.description: A fatal crash in Western Sydney on Sunday has prompted an investigation into how seatbelts were used by the passengers involved. A Subaru travelling on Stuart Road, Dharruk, near Mount Druitt, left th...

item.title: ENHYPEN Pop-up Store Opens in the Philippines for Fans
item.description: Fans of K-pop group ENHYPEN in the Philippines now have the chance to buy merchandise directly made by the artists themselves. ABS-CBN reports that a pop-up store featuring ENHYPEN-made items has open...

item.title: Canadians Discuss Provincial Trade Barriers, Education Funding, Airport Expansion, and French Language Learning
item.description: The Globe and Mail’s Letters to the Editor on March 22 covers a variety of issues affecting Canadians. A key topic is the reluctance of some provincial premiers to remove internal trade barriers, desp...

item.title: Iran warns of retaliatory attacks on US‑linked energy facilities after Kharg Island strikes
item.description: Iran has warned that it will launch retaliatory strikes on US‑linked energy and economic infrastructure if American forces keep attacking its oil and energy facilities. The threat comes after US milit...

item.title: SABC says programming decision behind end of ‘Face the Nation’, not politics
item.description: The South African Broadcasting Corporation (SABC) has rejected suggestions that political pressure from the Presidency or the ANC influenced its decision not to recommission the current affairs progra...

item.title: Two Decades After the First Tweet: How X, Musk, and Social Media Have Evolved
item.description: On March 21, 2006, Jack Dorsey sent the first-ever tweet: “just setting up my twittr.” Twenty years later, the platform—now called X—has undergone a dramatic evolution. Originally known as Twitter, th...

item.title: Why You Can’t Negotiate Lower Car Insurance Rates in Canada
item.description: In Canada, drivers cannot negotiate car insurance rates in the same way they might bargain for a cellphone plan. According to experts cited in The Globe and Mail, provincial regulations require each i...

item.title: Cuba Faces Third Nationwide Blackout in March Amid Fuel Shortages and Infrastructure Breakdown
item.description: Cuba experienced a nationwide power outage on Saturday, marking the third total blackout on the island during March 2026. According to the state-owned Cuban Electric Union, the cause of the outage has...

item.title: Qatari Military Helicopter Crashes During Routine Mission, Killing Six Crew Members
item.description: At least six individuals lost their lives when a Qatari military helicopter went down due to what authorities described as a 'technical malfunction.' According to Qatar’s Interior Ministry, the helico...

item.title: Susan Nakhumicha and George Natembeya Disagree Over Leadership of Trans Nzoia
item.description: In a fiery public exchange, former Health Cabinet Secretary Susan Nakhumicha and Trans Nzoia Governor George Natembeya clashed over leadership and the county's development. Nakhumicha, seeking to unse...

item.title: One Nation’s South Australian surge poses new challenges for Angus Taylor in Farrer byelection
item.description: One Nation’s strong showing in the recent South Australian state election has sent a warning signal to federal Liberal MP Angus Taylor as he prepares for the Farrer byelection on 9 May. In South Austr...

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs’ coach Kaze discusses Vilakazi’s progress and development path
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs co-coach Cedric Kaze has shed light on why 20-year-old attacking midfielder Mfundo 'Obrigado' Vilakazi has not been a regular starter this season. Despite limited starts, Vilakazi contin...

item.title: Punjab Minister Steps Down After Alleged Harassment Leads to Warehousing Official's Suicide
item.description: Punjab Transport Minister Laljit Singh Bhullar resigned on Saturday following the tragic death of Gagandeep Singh Randhawa, a district manager at the Punjab State Warehousing Corporation. Randhawa rep...

item.title: How Chittaprosad’s labour‑camp image reveals Mumbai’s changing urban fabric
item.description: This article discusses how the mid‑20th‑century woodcut or linocut Labour Camp by the artist Chittaprosad continues to speak to Mumbai’s present, even as the city has been transformed by redevelopment...

item.title: WRA Issues Flood Warning for Nairobi Estates as Dam Water Levels Increase
item.description: The Water Resources Authority (WRA) has sounded an urgent alarm to residents living downstream of the Nairobi Dam, warning them of possible flooding following continuous heavy rainfall. In a formal no...

item.title: How Morocco became Africa’s top arms importer amid regional rivalry with Algeria
item.description: Morocco has overtaken Algeria to become Africa’s leading importer of major arms between 2021 and 2025, according to the latest report by the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI). O...

item.title: Food prices in the UK may rise due to Middle East conflict, warns NFU
item.description: The National Farmers' Union (NFU) has warned that food prices in the UK are expected to increase as a result of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. According to NFU President Tom Bradshaw, vegeta...

item.title: Meteorite Fragment Damages Texas Home After Fiery Descent Over Houston
item.description: Residents across the Houston area were startled when a bright flash and loud boom filled the afternoon sky as a meteor entered Earth's atmosphere on Saturday. According to NASA, the meteor became visi...

item.title: Egerton University Offers Full Scholarships for Undergraduates and Postgraduates in Agriculture for East Africans
item.description: Egerton University has opened applications for fully funded scholarships under the TAGDev 2.0 programme for the 2026/2027 academic year. The scholarships are aimed at students from Kenya and other Eas...
